dimensions.name: Testability
dimensions.level_1: Acceptance criteria are missing or cannot be turned into automated tests.
dimensions.level_3: Most requirements are testable, but some edge cases or invariants are not materialized as falsifiable checks.
dimensions.level_5: All requirements, invariants, and boundary cases are expressed as observable behavior with direct test hooks.

dimensions.name: Ambiguity Free
dimensions.level_1: The spec uses vague language and leaves key behavior open to interpretation.
dimensions.level_3: Core behavior is concrete, but some wording still allows multiple implementations.
dimensions.level_5: The document is precise, falsifiable, and free from vague qualifiers.

dimensions.name: State Uniformity
dimensions.level_1: Success, error, empty, and boundary states are not covered or are internally inconsistent.
dimensions.level_3: Main states are covered, but one or more boundary contracts are not materialized in scenarios or AC.
dimensions.level_5: All applicable states and adversarial cases are covered consistently, including explicit non-applicable states.

dimensions.name: Precondition Hygiene
dimensions.level_1: External prerequisites are hidden inside implementation scope or grounding.
dimensions.level_3: Some prerequisites are visible, but stop conditions or scope boundaries are incomplete.
dimensions.level_5: Every external prerequisite is explicit, scoped correctly, and separated from the implementation contract.

dimensions.name: Grounding Realism
dimensions.level_1: The spec relies on files, dependencies, or runtime mechanisms that do not exist in the repository.
dimensions.level_3: The design is mostly grounded, but some stated implementation paths or guarantees are weakly evidenced.
dimensions.level_5: The design is tightly anchored to existing files, dependencies, and project patterns with no invented infrastructure.
